meaning: to be messy; to be dirty

expressions.english: to eat in a messy way

expressions.notes:
별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really
별로 안·(으)니·네요? = I don't think + S + be + that + adjective

expressions.english: This place is so dirty, I do not want to be here.
expressions.notes: 싫다 = to be not likable, to not like; to be unpleasant; to not enjoy, to hate

expressions.english: The room is really messy. Please tidy it up.
expressions.notes: 치우다 = to clean up, tidy up; to move

expressions.english: How can you live in a room this messy?
expressions.notes:
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective
어떻게 = how
살다 = to live

expressions.korean: 옷이 너무 지저분해서 옷 좀 갈아입고 갈게요.
expressions.english: I will come after I change my clothes since my clothes are too dirty.

expressions.korean: 눈 오는 날 신었더니 제 부츠가 지저분해졌어요.
expressions.english: My boots became dirty because I wore them on a snowy day.
expressions.notes:
눈 = snow
눈(이) 오다 = to snow
날 = day
신다 = to wear (footwear/socks)
부츠 = boots

expressions.korean: 미용실에 오래 못 갔더니 머리가 너무 지저분해요.
expressions.english: My hair is really messy because I was unable to go to the hair salon for a long time.
expressions.notes:
미용실 = hair salon
오래 = for a long time, a long time
못 = can't
머리 = head; hair

expressions.korean: 다른 자리는 괜찮은데 왜 경은 씨 자리만 지저분해요?
expressions.english: Why is it that Kyeong-eun's desk is messy while others' are clean?
expressions.notes:
다른 = other; another
자리 = space, spot, seat, position; occasion
괜찮다 = to be okay, to be alright; to be nice
-만 = only; just

expressions.korean: 방이 이렇게 지저분한데 어떻게 공부를 한다는 거예요?
expressions.english: How can you say that you can study when the room is this messy?
expressions.notes:
공부를 하다 = to study
어떻게·(느)·니·다는 거예요? = How can you say that you can/are going to + V?

expressions.korean: 제 동생은 아직 어려서 밥을 먹을 때 지저분하게 먹어요.
expressions.english:
My brother is still young, so when he is eating his meals, he is messy.
My brother is still young, so he's a messy eater.
expressions.notes:
동생 = younger sister/brother
어리다 = to be young

expressions.korean: 저기 선반 위가 너무 지저분한 것 같은데 같이 치울까요?
expressions.english: I think the top of the shelf is too dirty. Shall we clean it together?
expressions.notes:
저기 = there
선반 = shelf
위 = above; on; up
같이 = together; with + N
-(으)ㄹ까요? = Shall we + S + V?; Do you want me to + V?; Should we + V?; Let's + V.

expressions.korean: 서랍 속이 너무 지저분해서 안 쓰는 물 건은 모두 버렸어요.
expressions.english: Since the inside of my drawer was so messy, I threw out all the things I don't use.
expressions.notes:
서랍 = drawer (of desk or dresser)
속 = inside
모두 = all; every; everyone
버리다 = to throw away; to dump
